Review_Text: I did not have an enjoyable stay there. I booked a DELUXE QUEEN ROOM, which I know is a little bigger than the other room; unfortunately, I received a standard room with extremely limited space, and you can only go 2-3 steps in it. I am quite disappointed. I booked the deluxe queen room because the one shown on the picture appears to be much larger than the standard room. But when I checked in, they gave me precisely the same room as a standard size room. I confronted them and asked, "Is the room I'm getting really a deluxe room?" They said yes. I question why the room appears to be the same size as the standard room. Then they show me the other room of the DELUXE ROOM, and indeed, the other deluxe room is WAYYYYY more SPACEYYYYY than the one I are given. So I asked if I could change rooms, and they said they were fully booked. Then they asked if I was staying for two nights. And after I said yes, they said they'd change me to the other room the next day, but they didn't even contact me the next day. which means I have to live in that small room with my niece. There is so little space for a two-person live-in that, once again, I highlight that I am aware that a standard room is small; that is why I BOOKED THE DELUXE ROOM! This is a fraud. Scam in a legal way. It's unfair, you know, since I paid for the deluxe size room, but they put me in the standard size room, which they said was the deluxe room because the bed was a queen bed and the room had a window??? What's the logic? But the room fucking little !! . Oh, my God.

Review_Text: Check-in was smooth, but everything went downhill after that. I booked a Queen Bed room, expecting a comfortable stay in the Bukit Bintang area. However, it was a huge red flag the moment I entered the room - it was extremely small, had no windows, and the walls were so thin that I could hear every noise from the rooms next door. The receptionist even confirmed that none of the rooms have windows, which was never mentioned during booking. The biggest disappointment was housekeeping. I stayed for two nights, but the room was never cleaned on the second day. When I requested a clean towel, the receptionist said they had no towels available because the hotel was overbooked. After I insisted, she told me she would “get one from another hotel,” but I waited over 10 minutes and received nothing. At that point, I decided to check out early. The place felt unprofessional and misleading. I later discovered they also operate Sungei Wang Hotel and Kingston Hotel, so beware — they seem to manage multiple properties under different names. I strongly advise avoiding this hotel and its sister properties. It’s poorly managed, uncomfortable, and absolutely not worth the money.

Review_Text: This apartment is suitable for anyone who looking for a spacious place, walking distance to Bukit Bintang area, especially TRX Exchange. Alot of shopping, food area within a short walking distance. Its literally 5-10 minutes walk to TRX and Bukit Bintang areas, however, there is no dedicated area for pedestrian on the way to TRX. But to Bukit Bintang, it is convenience. We got 59th floor unit on our stay, just below the roof top 60th floor. Its so high that the wait and time at the elevator is not as convenient as you can take more than 10 mins just to wait for the lift and going down to the lobby. Would suggest communicate in advance to the guest to check their comfort level, or can also price it differently in the expense of convenience. In anyway, we don;t see any "view" or other value to stay in the high floor. The unit itself, marketed as japandi design, quite spacious living room, but very small - no window - second bedroom. On the day we arrived, it was heavy rain and there was water leakage from the window in the living room. Apartment facility is pretty complete, washing machine, full kitchen, large fridge, super high speed internet. However, during our stay, they washing machine was broken. On cleanliness, hope they can regularly replace the bed sheet (it was brown color bedsheet due to the japandi design), while it may be cleaned but can easily see stained on the brown color bedsheet. Last point, I think they need to better invest on the bed mattress on the main bedroom. I believe people come for the sleeping comfort. Can clearly say it was a cheap mattress, can feel the spring when you sleep on it. A decent mattress would definitely improve guest comfort.

Will definitely stay here again if I come back to Bukit Bintang in the future.
